revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕神社 (土浦市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Atago Shrine | image = File:Atago-Jinja (Tsuchiura).JPG | location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsuchiura|en|Tsuchiura|qid=Q653446|lt=Tsuchiura|1=Tsuchiura}}, {{ill|Shimo-Takatsu|ja|下高津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Takatsu|1=Shimo-Takatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361513}} 2-chome 8-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.07411111111111|N|140.18947222222224|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} | Former Rank = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Founded = | Map = Japan #Japan Ibaraki | location = {{ill|Tsuchiura|qid=Q653446|1=Tsuchiura}} }} '''Atago Shrine''' (愛宕神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Shimo-Takatsu, Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki Prefecture'''. During the Edo period, it was known as '''Atago Gongen'''<ref name="gongen" />. The shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)''' under the former shrine Ranking system. == Overview == Atago Shrine is situated '''along the former Mito Kaidō (now National Route 354 old road)''', on a '''hillside overlooking the right bank of the Sakura River'''. It was once '''a scenic spot offering views of Lake Kasumigaura''', and its sacred lanterns were said to '''serve as a beacon'''<ref name="map" />. The shrine entrance faces east, along the former Mito Kaidō, and is accessible via a '''staircase with handrails'''<ref name="kaidan" />. Another entrance is located at '''Jōfuku-ji Temple (常福寺)''' at the back. The shrine grounds are closely surrounded by '''a parking lot and a cemetery'''. A '''designated parking area''' for shrine visitors is located '''across the road''', next to the '''Shimo-Takatsu Milestone (下高津の道標, built in 1733), a designated historical site''' of Tsuchiura City. During the '''Edo period''', it served as both the '''{{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} of Shimo-Takatsu Village''' and the '''protector of the omote-kimon (front demon gate) of Tsuchiura Castle'''. Near the '''Zeni-Kamebashi Bridge''', at the '''Ōmachi intersection''', stood the '''Kōzu Gate (南門) of Tsuchiura Castle'''. == Deities == * '''{{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} (軻遇突知命)''' – The deity of '''fire protection'''. * The '''shrine’s sacred object''' is a '''statue of Shōgun Jizō (勝軍地蔵)''', the Buddhist equivalent of Kagutsuchi<ref name="kaisetsu" />. * It is widely revered as a '''fire prevention deity'''. * It is unclear whether this shrine has merged with other shrines as part of shrine reorganization policies<ref name="ibaraki" /><ref name="gappei" />. === Auxiliary Shrines === Within the shrine grounds, four auxiliary shrines are enshrined: * '''Katori Shrine''' (経津主命, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}) * '''Tenman Shrine''' (Sugawara no Michizane, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}) * '''Fuji Shrine''' (木花咲耶姫命, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * '''Inari Shrine''' (倉稲魂命, {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}), also known as '''Takatsu Inari Shrine (高津稲荷神社)'''. === Festivals === A '''[[Gion and Tenno Festivals|Gion Festival]]''' is held in the summer, featuring '''two or three festival floats (dashi) each year'''. == History == The shrine was founded during the '''Tengei era (938-947)''' by '''Taira no Sadamori''' to '''pray for victory''' in battle<ref name="sinra" />. During the '''Sengoku period''', it was revered by '''Sugaya Iyonokami Katsusada''', the lord of Tsuchiura Castle, who also '''organized the shrine festival'''. In '''1669 (Kanbun 9)''', '''Tsuchiya Kazunao''', the new '''lord of Tsuchiura Castle''', established Atago Shrine as the '''protector of the castle’s omote-kimon''' and rebuilt its structures<ref name="kaisetsu" />. Some records suggest the '''shrine's reconstruction was completed in 1679 (Enpō 7)'''<ref name="map" />. In '''1699 (Genroku 12)''', the shrine was '''destroyed by fire''' but was rebuilt in '''1811 (Bunka 8)'''. The '''main ridge of the roof''' features the '''Tsuchiya clan's family crest, the Mitsuishi-mon (三ツ石紋)'''<ref name="kaisetsu" />. In '''1882 (Meiji 15)''', the shrine was '''officially Ranked as a village shrine'''. == Cultural Properties == '''Atago Shrine Main Worship Hall (本拝殿)''' * '''Designated as a Tsuchiura City Cultural Property on March 1, 2001 (Heisei 13)'''. * Features a '''thatched hip-and-gable (irimoya) roof''' in a '''combined shrine hall structure (複合社殿)'''. * Thatched-roof shrine structures are '''rare in Tsuchiura''', and a '''plaque listing donors''' for the '''roof maintenance project''' is displayed inside the shrine<ref name="kaisetsu" />. == Nearby Landmarks == * '''{{ill|Jōfuku-ji (Tsuchiura)|ja|常福寺 (土浦市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōfuku-ji (Tsuchiura)|1=Jōfuku-ji Temple (Tsuchiura)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481607}}''' * '''Kasumigaura Medical Center''', part of the '''National Hospital Organization'''. == Other Atago Shrines in Tsuchiura == '''Atago Shrine (Nishi-Manabe Town)'''<ref name="nishimanabe" /> * '''location:''' Nishi-Manabe Town, Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki Prefecture (西真鍋町4-47). * '''deity:''' Kagutsuchi (軻遇突知命). * '''Founded:''' Unknown. Former '''unranked shrine'''. The grounds include the '''Manabe Atago Shrine Kofun (ancient Kofun)''' and the '''Nishi-Manabe Ruins'''. * The '''front approach''' faces '''National Route 125''', but the '''stone steps were damaged''' by the '''2011 Great East Japan Earthquake''' and '''are now barricaded'''. However, the shrine is still accessible '''via Jinnei-ji Temple (真延寺) or a back entrance through the fields'''. '''Atago Shrine (Koyamazaki Town)'''<ref name="koyamazaki" /> * '''location:''' Koyamazaki Town, Tsuchiura City, Ibaraki Prefecture (小山崎町623). * '''deity:''' Kagutsuchi (軻遇突知命). * '''Founded:''' 1761 (Hōreki 11). Former '''unranked shrine'''. '''Ichiemon Iwase''' from '''Dōchi Village''' established the shrine as '''a clan deity''', enshrining '''Atago Shrine from Kasama''', later relocating it to the present site<ref name="koyamazaki" />. * Unlike the '''urban shrines''' in '''Shimo-Takatsu''' or '''Nishi-Manabe''', this shrine is located '''deep in farmland''', surrounded by '''a dense sacred forest'''. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * '''Nakayama Nobuna & Kurita Hiroshi''', *Shinpen Hitachi Koku-shi*, Sekizenkan, 1899-1901. * '''Ibaraki Newspaper''', *Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Album*, 1942. * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Database (Shinra)''' – [http://db.niihari.jp/index.php] * '''Tsuchiura City Official Website''' – [http://www.city.tsuchiura.lg.jp] * '''Tsuchiura City Board of Education''', *Atago Shrine Cultural Properties*, 2013. {{Shinto navbox|qq=}} {{List of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Ibaraki-pref-stub|qq=}} {{Atago Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|愛宕神社 (土浦市)|version=91346504|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:愛宕神社 (土浦市)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11493858}} [[Category:Buildings in Tsuchiura]] [[Category:History of Tsuchiura]] [[Category:Atago shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
